localPath.description: Absolute path to the pod-local payload-store directory.

metadataCacheIdentifier.description: TYPO3 cache frontend identifier that stores the central metadata. The consumer configures this frontend and picks the backend themselves (e.g. KeyValueBackend, Typo3DatabaseBackend, etc.).

maxPayloadBytes.description: Upper bound for both writes (rejected with InvalidDataException) and decompressed reads (zstd-bomb mitigation). Capped at 64 MiB by the schema: ext-zstd pre-allocates the full decompressed size before honouring the post-check, so a generous cap is a DoS vector — even a 50-byte malicious zstd stream can decompress to multiple gigabytes. If your workload legitimately stores entries larger than 64 MiB you almost certainly want a different cache architecture.

minCompressedBytes.description: Payloads smaller than this size are stored uncompressed on disk (a one-byte marker indicates the on-disk codec). Skip-compress avoids the gzdeflate/zstd_compress fixed cost on tiny values like rootline or hash entries. Set to 0 to always compress.

payloadL1MaxEntries.description: Maximum number of decompressed payloads kept in the per-request in-memory L1 cache. 0 disables payload memoization (only the metadata-L1 stays active). The L1 is request-scoped — FrankenPHP creates a fresh backend instance per request, so this cap applies per request, not globally. Hot caches like extbase's ClassSchemata typically need only a handful of entries; raise this when a cache has many hot identifiers (Fluid template carousel, partials).

payloadL1MaxBytes.description: Soft memory budget for the payload L1 in bytes (default 4 MB). Entries that would push the total above this are evicted in insertion order. A single payload larger than this limit bypasses the L1 entirely (rather than evicting everything else). 0 disables the bytes-cap; the entry count then becomes the only limit.

backendVersionEnvVar.description: Name of the environment variable that supplies a deploy-scoped identifier (e.g. image tag, commit SHA, release version). The value is folded into the cache-entry hash so that each deploy automatically invalidates previous cache entries. Set to a variable that changes per deploy in your CI/CD (default: IMAGE_TAG).
